question: Which word is 'another word for notice' (verb)?

choices: soft and smooth like the material silk

question: Which adjective describes something 'covered in small, short pointed spines'?

question: Which word describes 'something difficult to detect' in terms of smell?

You can then move on to different galleries that take you through Jordan's history in chronological order, with a look at more than 2,000 artifacts.' What does the collection cover?
choices: Only the modern history of Jordan
Jordan's history from the Stone Age to the present
The history of Amman city only

question: Read the passage: 'These are explained using modern graphics and interactive displays which are absolutely fascinating.' How are the museum themes explained?
Read the passage: 'I happened to come across this gem of a museum last week on a family trip to Jordan, so we decided to check it out.' How did the writer find the Jordan Museum?
choices: on a family trip to Jordan
because it was on the news

question: I'd rather not ________ out tonight.
Choose the correct sentence.
choices: I'd rather you didn't tell me what to order.
I'd rather you don't tell me what to order.
I'd rather you to not tell me what to order.
I'd rather you not telling me what to order.

question: Choose the sentence that expresses a general preference using 'prefer'.
choices: I would prefer to eat at home tonight.
I prefer eating out to cooking at home.
I'd rather eat at home this evening.

question: 'I don't want to eat too late.' → I'd rather ________.
'I like home-cooked meals more than takeaways.' → I prefer ________.
'I really don't mind.' → I'm ________.

Although it's still a bit strange, Kamal ________ living in the student halls.
When my sister was a child, she ________ with dolls.

choices: B — This is slowly becoming normal
P — This was true in the past but no longer

question: 'Visitors used to be able to make out the sea from here.' This situation means:
choices: B — This is slowly becoming normal
P — This was true in the past but no longer

choices: clues in the text and background knowledge

question: Read the passage: 'The school taught her to come to terms with being blind.' The statement 'She felt better when she changed schools because she no longer felt isolated' is:
choices: False — the text states she was still unhappy
True — implied because the school taught her to come to terms with her blindness
False — the text directly says she was unhappy

question: Read the passage: 'In November 1801 I decided to leave London and move to the country. I went up to the north of England and visited a house on the Yorkshire moors. I arranged to visit the owner of Thrushcross Grange, a man called Heathcliff.' In what year does Mr Lockwood's story begin?
Read the passage: 'I went up to the north of England and visited a house on the Yorkshire moors.' Where is Thrushcross Grange located?

Read the passage: 'Heathcliff and I had hardly spoken on my first visit. I had not stayed long because he was rude. But I was interested in getting to know the man, so I decided to go back to Wuthering Heights the next day.' Why did Lockwood go back to Wuthering Heights?
choices: He was interested in getting to know Heathcliff better.

question: Read the passage: 'All's well that ends well.' What does this Shakespearean quote mean?
choices: Only the ending of a story matters, not how it started.
If the outcome is good, it doesn't matter what happened before.
Things do not always end well.
Every beginning has a good ending.

question: Read the passage: 'He interacted with people from all backgrounds and classes and his staging of plays brought theatre to the general public.' Who did Shakespeare bring theatre to?
